figures.caption: FIG. 8. Same as the previous figure, but for λ = 0.5 and hz = 0.4.

FIG. 7. Evolution of spin-resolved localization and delocalization characteristics across the eigenstates, quantified by IPR↑, IPR↓, NPR↑, and NPR↓, at λ = 0.8 and hz = 0.8 for four interaction strengths U .
FIG. 10. IPRmax and IPRmin phase diagrams in the hz–U plane for λ = 0.5 (top) and λ = 0.8 (bottom).

FIG. 2. Combined spin-resolved mean-field eigenvalue spectrum as a function of the Hubbard interaction U . For each value of U , all spin-up and spin-down eigenvalues obtained from the self-consistent Hartree mean-field decoupling are merged into a single set and plotted together. In the first column, we fix hz = 0.5 and vary λ from 0.5 to 0.8 in panels (a) and (c). In the second column, λ is fixed at 0.5, while hz is varied from 0.4 to 0.8 in panels (b) and (d), respectively.

FIG. 3. Spin-resolved energy splitting ∆E = E↑ − E↓ as a function of U , with the color scale representing |IPR↑ − IPR↓|, shown for the same parameter-space orientation as in the preceding figure, highlighting the onset of interactiondriven spin-dependent localization.

FIG. 14. Spatiotemporal evolution of the probability density |ψ(t)|2 across the lattice, resolved for both spin sectors. Panels (a)–(d) illustrate the extended, intermediate, localized, and intermediate dynamical regimes obtained at interaction strengths U = 1, 5, 7, and 9, respectively, for λ = 0.8 and hz = 0.8. The system size is fixed at L = 144 to enhance the visibility of the time evolution and to clearly distinguish extended from localized propagation in both the up- and downspin channels.

FIG. 16. Maximum wave-packet spread σG,max (crimson solid line) and survival probability Pr at r = 10 (blue dashed line) plotted as functions of U for four representative λ–hz parameter sets, as specified in Fig. 2.

FIG. 12. (Color online). Dependence of various mean-field observables on U : variance (a), ASD (b), double occupancy D (c), 〈(n↑ − n↓) 2〉 (d), △ (e), and local entropy S (f), shown for λ = 0.5 and hz = 0.4.

FIG. 4. Figure shows the interaction-driven evolution of spin-resolved eigenstate profiles for λ = 0.5, hz = 0.4 (top row) and λ = 0.8, hz = 0.8 (bottom row). Panels (c) and (d) display ∆IPR, with the color scale capped at 0.20 for enhanced visibility, consistent with the IPR-resolved spectral analysis.

FIG. 5. Variation of the average inverse participation ratio (AIPR) and average normalized participation ratio (ANPR) as functions of U for four representative λ–hz parameter sets, corresponding to the parameter regimes shown in Fig. 2.

FIG. 6. Dependence of the up- and down-spin sector differences in the average inverse participation ratio and average normalized participation ratio (△AIPR and △ANPR) on U for four representative parameter sets.
